operating_state: Total revenues increased to $11.3 billion in Q3 2021 from $4.4 billion in Q3 2020, driven by higher commodity prices and the Concho acquisition completed January 15, 2021. Production reached 1,544 MBOED, increasing 477 MBOED year-over-year, with Concho contributing approximately 343 MBOED. Average realized price increased to $56.92 per BOE compared with $30.94 per BOE in the prior year quarter.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased reflecting the expanded asset base from Concho and higher activity levels. Depreciation, depletion and amortization increased driven by higher production volumes partially offset by lower rates from price-related reserve revisions. The company recognized transaction and restructuring costs related to Concho integration.
material_events: ConocoPhillips completed the $13.1 billion all-stock acquisition of Concho Resources on January 15, 2021, issuing 285.9 million shares and assuming debt. The company announced a $9.5 billion agreement to acquire Shell's Permian assets in September 2021, expected to close in Q4 2021. ConocoPhillips repurchased $2.2 billion of common stock during nine months and increased quarterly dividend 7% to $0.46 per share.
macro_exposures: The company faces commodity price volatility exposure with revenues directly tied to WTI crude and natural gas pricing, benefiting from significantly higher realized prices compared to prior year. Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in negative impact for nine months 2021. Venezuelan expropriation proceedings continue with an ICSID award pending enforcement following the lifting of the stay in September 2021.
forward_outlook: Management expects the Shell Permian acquisition to close by January 18, 2022 subject to regulatory approval. Fourth-quarter 2021 production expected to be 1.53 to 1.57 MMBOED excluding Libya and pending acquisitions. The company maintains revolving credit facility and targets disposition proceeds by 2023. ConocoPhillips committed to reducing gross debt by $5 billion over five years.
uncertainty_profile: The Shell Permian acquisition remains subject to regulatory approval with deposit at risk if ConocoPhillips materially breaches representations or covenants. Multiple climate change lawsuits across several states seek unspecified damages with unprecedented legal theories creating uncertain exposure. Alaska Willow project approval was vacated by federal judge in August 2021, with the company continuing FEED work while engaging with BLM on path forward.
